name: F12-revert-validation-P6-salvage
purpose: Validate the revert of F12 (Write re-allowed) actually restores baseline conflict-agent performance, vs the 49min/$19.22 measured under F12.
scenario: P6 inventory CLI bench, manual salvage merge of 3 failed-cert improve branches with --no-certify. Same intents as the original P6 baseline.
config: Write ALLOWED (revert applied). disallowed_tools = ['Bash'] only. Original merger-conflict.md prompt restored.
tool_usage: agent used 'Write' as primary tool (no Edit calls visible in conflict-agent.log)

revert_Write_allowed_P6_salvage_THIS_RUN.note: Excludes 1 clean merge of imp-alerts which took ~5 min; per-conflict-branch numbers normalized.

comparison.verdict: Write-allowed is 2.6x cheaper per branch and 1.4x faster per branch than F12. Revert validated.

caveats: Different LLM rolls produce different code complexity each run. This is real data but not perfectly controlled.
Both runs had 0 validation retries, so the 'cold-start retry penalty' hypothesis (about agent-mode helping) wasn't tested either way.
The improve agent durations (cost~$3.5/improve, ~11min) were similar across runs — improve cost is not the variable here.
